subject: [PATCH v5 35/36] KVM: guest_memfd: bind backing memory to a NUMA node at creation

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new flag, GUEST_MEMFD_FLAG_BIND_NODE, to the KVM guest memory file descriptor (memfd) API. When this flag is set, the kernel binds the backing memory of the memfd to a specified NUMA node at creation time, ensuring that all allocated pages are on the same node without requiring userspace to use mbind(). The patch also adds support for this flag in the KVM_CAP_GUEST_MEMFD_FLAGS capability and updates the include files accordingly. This change is only enabled when CONFIG_NUMA is set.
